title: AI in Class: Use It or Lose It
modules: Mapping the AI Tools Teachers Use
When AI Helps Learning — and When It Doesn't
Bias, Errors, and the Trust Test
synopsis: You are a student adviser helping a school board decide which AI tools belong in classrooms and which ones should be banned — and why. This course builds the critical vocabulary and evaluative skills to assess AI tools the way educators, ethicists, and students all need to.
rationale: Teenagers are already using AI tools for schoolwork and teachers are adopting them without clear frameworks, yet students have almost no structured opportunity to critically evaluate these tools or participate in the policies that govern them.
learning_outcome: Students can audit at least two classroom AI tools for accuracy, bias, and pedagogical value, and produce a written policy recommendation a real school board could debate.
source_signals: Learning about artificial intelligence: A hub of MIT resources for K-12 students - MIT Media Lab
As AI enters Pennsylvania classrooms, teachers and students face a learning curve - City & State Pennsylvania
Arizona Schools Lead In Usage Of AI As A Learning Tool - AZ FREE NEWS
